item.title: Weeds and nettles are once again starting to obstruct the footpath, 10th May
item.description:
As above, there is no maintenance done on the footpath unless reported.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brooklyn Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blue wheelie bin left on the pavement, 10th May
item.description:
This bin has been on the pavement at the corner of Glenister Road and Glenforth Street for 3/4 weeks. It’s clearly too heavy for regular collection and seems to be full of non recyclable items. Can you kindly remove.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glenforth Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Clothes fly tipped in Beatty Road, 10th May
item.description:

item.title: Unnecessary road sign, 10th May
item.description:
A 'loose chippings' sign has recently been attached to a lamp post outside 259 Chislehurst Road. There seems no reason for it to be there. Can it please be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree in decline: arborist inspection needed, 10th May
item.description:
I’m concerned about the declining condition of the large conifer in Crouch Close. It has been a familiar and valued part of the area for many years, but over the last year or two it has developed increasingly yellowing/browning foliage and noticeable dead upper growth. In May, this tree has always previously appeared deep green and vigorous, whereas now substantial sections of the canopy appear yellow-green or brown. Recently a substantial branch failed and required urgent attention. Over the years I have also observed quite significant movement/heaving of the ground around the base during periods of high wind, although at that time the tree otherwise appeared healthy. Since then, however, the tree’s overall condition appears to have declined noticeably compared with its appearance over the past 17 years. Given the tree’s size and proximity to homes, parked vehicles and pedestrian areas, I would be grateful if it could be inspected by an arboricultural officer. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crouch Close, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pond has dried up, 10th May
item.description:
Pond has dried out causing harm to wildlife and enjoyment of the park
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tetty Way, Plaistow, Greater London

item.description:
Looks like it’s not been cleaned in a while

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Road, Penge, Greater London

item.title: 2 rubbish bags from Council cleaning have been left for a couple of weeks and now people adding to the pile with more rubbish, 10th May
item.description:
The bags and rubbish are between 146 and 148 Tottenham Road. Please could the rubbish be removed and the street cleaned.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tottenham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The obligatory Sunday shit, 10th May
item.description:
Every single weekend the commercial waste from Micky’s chippy is left out on Saturday night and by Sunday is ripped to shreds with disgusting litter all over the pavement. Come on Hackney, please do something about this!!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pellerin Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned boat and trailer, 10th May
item.description:
A boat and trailer has been abandoned / fly tipper on the road over night and looks to be unstable and is not securely attached to any vehicle. Maybe worth checking neighbours ring door bell footage to enable you to prosecute the fly tipper.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 58, Eden Park Avenue, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
